rounds.blurb: Dorothea Lange's "Migrant Mother": Florence Owens Thompson in a pea pickers' camp in Nipomo, California.
rounds.credit: Dorothea Lange / Farm Security Administration — public domain
rounds.story: Florence Owens Thompson, 32 and a mother of seven, sat in a pea pickers' camp where the crop had frozen. Lange stopped for ten minutes and made six exposures; the last became the defining image of the Great Depression.

rounds.blurb: Martin Luther King Jr. at the March on Washington for Jobs and Freedom.
rounds.credit: Rowland Scherman / U.S. Information Agency — public domain
rounds.story: A quarter-million people marched on Washington for jobs and freedom. Near the end of the program, singer Mahalia Jackson called out 'Tell them about the dream, Martin' — and King set aside his prepared text.

rounds.blurb: Sally Ride on Challenger's flight deck during STS-7 — America's first woman in space.
rounds.story: Chosen from NASA's first astronaut class open to women, Ride spent the run-up fielding press questions about whether she would cry in orbit. Aboard STS-7 she operated the robot arm she had helped design.

rounds.blurb: A carpooling appeal near Vancouver, Washington, as the first oil crisis dragged on.
rounds.credit: David Falconer / EPA Documerica, National Archives — public domain
rounds.story: OPEC's embargo quadrupled oil prices in months; America answered with odd-even gas rationing, a 55 mph national speed limit, and signs like this one. The era of the cheap-gas land yacht never came back.

rounds.blurb: Fireboats battle the Deepwater Horizon blaze in the Gulf of Mexico.
rounds.story: The blowout killed 11 workers and sank the rig two days later; the well then leaked for 87 days, some four million barrels — the largest marine oil spill in history. Penalties eventually passed $60 billion.
